title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers response bytes, content codings, character encodings, and source identity into a changed byte-to-Unicode admission ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of byte-to-Unicode admission ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
Validate and apply declared content codings in order while distinguishing unsupported, corrupt, partial, and over-budget representations.
Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Compressed Byte Budget
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 3811 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Source Digest Provenance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-source-digest-provenance-pxubvw" that uses case 4381 to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Source Digest Provenance.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Source Digest Provenance into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Source Digest Provenance, keep Source Digest Provenance connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-source-digest-provenance-pxubvw
# Competency: scraper-source-digest-provenance.
# Case 4381: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_source_digest_provenance_pxubvw(wire_bytes: int = 48000, expanded_bytes: int = 120000, byte_budget: int = 250000, decoded_pages: int = 6, visible_decode_failures: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pxubvw = "pxubvw-wvbuxp-4381"
    assert evidence_variant_pxubvw
    evidence_axes_pxubvw = (
        "authorizationpxubvw",
        "url4381",
        "responsewvbuxp",
        "treepxubvw4381",
        "frontier4381pxubvw",
        "reportwvbuxp4381",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pxubvw)) == 6
    if wire_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < wire_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "byte-accounting"
    if expanded_bytes > byte_budget:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "expansion-budget"
    if decoded_pages < 1:
        return False, decoded_pages, "decode-coverage"
    if visible_decode_failures < 1:
        return False, visible_decode_failures, "decode-evidence"
    assert expanded_bytes <= byte_budget
    return True, expanded_bytes, "bounded-decoding-ledger"
